theories.label: sociocultural and poststructural approaches
theories.summary: This cluster treats personality and subjectivity as historically and relationally produced through discourse, power, norm, and social practice.
theories.course_role: Carries the strongest late-course challenge to inner-essence models of personality.
lecture_titles: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)

related_theories.rationale: Both explain subjectivity socially, though they emphasise different mechanisms.
The discursive and cultural turn opens into narrative accounts of identity and meaning.

representative_excerpts: Forelæsningen behandler køn som en psykosocial proces frem for et fast persontræk.
Kilderne viser, hvordan femininitet og maskulinitet formes i samspillet mellem intime erfaringer og institutionelle rammer.
Kilderne samler poststrukturalistiske perspektiver på etik, subjektivering og magt.
Foucault omformulerer etik som praksisser for selvforhold i historiske magtrelationer.

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Poststrukturalisme, diskursiv psykologi og feministisk teori (Forelæsning 20, 2026-04-20)
representative_excerpts: Forelæsningen samler socialkonstruktionistiske bidrag til personlighedsforståelse.
Kilderne kritiserer individualistiske modeller og fremhæver, at psykologiske realiteter skabes relationelt i sprog og praksis.

theories.label: psychoanalytic personality theory
theories.summary: This cluster explains personality through conflict, development, unconscious meaning, and layered psychic organisation.
theories.course_role: Forms the course's first large interpretive theory bloc and a major alternative to trait and biosocial models.

related_theories.rationale: Phenomenology shifts attention from hidden psychic depth to lived experience.
Humanistic psychology resists deficit-heavy and control-oriented images of the person.

representative_excerpts: Kilderne samler klassiske psykoanalytiske formuleringer om drift, konflikt og personlighedsstruktur.
Freudianske tekster læses som en dynamisk model, hvor ubevidste processer organiserer symptomer og handling.
Laplanche introduces a historical-structural reading of Freud that treats contradictions in psychoanalytic theory as theoretically productive rather than accidental.
He argues that tensions in concepts like drive, ego, and object should be interpreted dialectically as clues to the object of psychoanalysis.
The text positions psychoanalytic theory as evolving through conceptual crises tied to both clinical and metapsychological problems.
The text also exposes methodological and ethical tensions in early psychoanalytic case construction.
Ricoeur examines how psychoanalysis can justify truth claims when its data are interpretive, transferential, and bound to the analytic situation.
He argues that standard natural-science verification models are insufficient for psychoanalytic theory and practice.
Forelæsningen samler nyere psykoanalytiske teorier om, hvordan subjektet konstitueres gennem drifter, objektrelationer og det ubevidste.
Kilderne viser både, hvordan Freuds metapsykologi videreudvikles, og hvordan spørgsmålet om det ubevidstes status fortsat organiserer teoridannelsen.

theories.label: trait and assessment psychology
theories.summary: This cluster treats personality as patterned individual differences that can be assessed, compared, and modelled across persons.
theories.course_role: Provides the early-course baseline that many later traditions criticise, qualify, or reinterpret.
lecture_titles: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)

related_theories.rationale: State and situation perspectives complicate purely static trait thinking.
Critical personalism resists reducing the person to scale profiles.
Narrative approaches replace variable profiles with temporal self-interpretation.

representative_excerpts: Personality assessment (Forelæsning 2, 2026-02-03)
Mayer and Bryan discuss frameworks for evaluating personality assessment quality and scope.
This article addresses contemporary challenges in personality assessment and construct interpretation.

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Personality traits, personality states, and personality dimensions (Forelæsning 3, 2026-02-09)
representative_excerpts: Forelæsningen afklarer forholdet mellem træk, tilstande og overordnede personlighedsdimensioner.
Kilderne viser, at stabilitet afhænger af tidsskala og kontekst frem for én fast antagelse.

theories.label: critical psychology
theories.summary: This cluster explains subjectivity, action, and difficulty through social practice, historical conditions, and collective possibilities for change.
theories.course_role: Forms the course's most explicit critique of individualising psychology.

related_theories.rationale: Critical psychology moves from person-level caution to a broader critique of social conditions.
Both theorise subjectivity socially, but via different vocabularies of practice, discourse, and power.

representative_excerpts: Critical psychology (Forelæsning 16, 2026-03-24)
Forelæsningen samler kritisk psykologi med fokus på subjektivitet, samfundsforhold og handlemuligheder.
Holzkamp udvikler et grundlag for kritisk psykologi ved at sætte subjektivitet, samfundsmæssige vilkår og handlemuligheder i centrum.
Kritisk psykologi (Forelæsning 17, 2026-04-07)
Forelæsningen kobler praksisteori med kritisk psykologi i en relationel forståelse af subjektivitet og forandring.
Grundbog kapitel 10 - Kritisk psykologi

theories.label: existential psychology
theories.summary: This cluster frames personality through freedom, relation, anxiety, finitude, and the person's stance toward existence.
theories.course_role: Deepens the post-phenomenological block by foregrounding existential conditions of personhood.

related_theories.rationale: Existential psychology builds on phenomenology's experiential method.
Both emphasise personhood, but existential psychology centres conflict, finitude, and freedom more strongly.

representative_excerpts: Eksistentiel personlighedspsykologi I (Funch) (Forelæsning 12, 2026-03-10)
Forelæsningen samler eksistentielle perspektiver på personlighed, angst og frihed.
Lidelse kan beskrives som begrænsede eksistentielle muligheder.
Eksistentiel personlighedspsykologi II (Bjerregaard-Nielsen) (Forelæsning 13, 2026-03-16)
Kilderne samler eksistentielle og fænomenologiske bidrag til moderne personlighedsforståelse.
Eksistentielle temaer organiserer mange kliniske problemstillinger.

theories.label: biosocial personality perspectives
theories.summary: This cluster explains personality through culture, evolution, and genetics while preserving questions about interpretation, environment, and scope.
theories.course_role: Supplies a strongly explanatory block before the course turns toward more interpretive and critical traditions.

related_theories.rationale: Biosocial approaches add explanatory depth to individual-difference description.
Later sociocultural approaches relocate explanation from biology to practice, discourse, and power.

representative_excerpts: Culture, evolution, genetics, and personality (Forelæsning 5, 2026-02-16)
This review synthesizes evidence that personality differences are shaped by many small genetic and environmental influences rather than single dominant causes.
Operationalization choices influence observed genetic and environmental effects.

theories.label: narrative psychology
theories.summary: This cluster explains personality through story, temporality, cultural meaning, and the ongoing revision of identity.
theories.course_role: Provides the clearest late-course alternative to trait and mechanism-oriented models.

related_theories.rationale: Narrative psychology foregrounds story and interpretation rather than dimensional scoring.
Narrative work inherits the course's cultural and discursive sensitivity to identity formation.

representative_excerpts: Kilderne introducerer narrativ psykologi, hvor personlighed forstås gennem fortælling, tid og kulturel meningsdannelse.
Forelæsningen kontrasterer narrative perspektiver med træk- og mekanismeorienterede modeller.

theories.label: comparative theory analysis
theories.summary: This cluster uses historical and comparative reflection to judge what each theory can and cannot explain about persons, selves, and subjectivity.
theories.course_role: Makes theory choice itself into an explicit object of learning at the end of the course.
lecture_titles: Sammenligning af teorier og subjektets historie og eksamen (Forelæsning 22, 2026-04-27)

related_theories.rationale: The final lecture evaluates the scope and limitations of the early differential frameworks.
The final lecture treats psychoanalysis as one major explanatory option among several.
Late-course sociocultural traditions become part of the final contrastive synthesis.

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Sammenligning af teorier og subjektets historie og eksamen (Forelæsning 22, 2026-04-27)
representative_excerpts: Sammenligning af teorier og subjektets historie og eksamen (Forelæsning 22, 2026-04-27)

theories.label: humanistic psychology
theories.summary: This cluster treats persons as oriented toward growth, authenticity, relation, and context-sensitive unfolding rather than deficit alone.
theories.course_role: Provides the course's clearest growth-oriented alternative to reductive person models.

related_theories.rationale: Humanistic psychology rejects more deficit-focused and control-oriented framings of the person.

representative_excerpts: Humanistisk psykologi (Forelæsning 14, 2026-03-17)
Forelæsningen behandler humanistisk psykologi som et alternativ til reduktionistiske personmodeller.
Humanistisk psykologi udfordrer underskuds- og kontrolmodeller.

theories.label: phenomenological psychology
theories.summary: This cluster treats personality through lived experience, embodiment, and the person's situated relation to world and meaning.
theories.course_role: Starts the course's turn toward first-person and experience-near approaches.

related_theories.rationale: Existential psychology radicalises the experiential turn around freedom and finitude.
Both privilege experience, meaning, and personhood over reductionism.

representative_excerpts: This text applies phenomenological analysis to contemporary questions of selfhood and psychological understanding.
Phenomenological method complements but does not mirror natural-science designs.
Spinelli presents an existential-phenomenological framework for understanding personhood and therapeutic encounter.

theories.label: critical personalism
theories.summary: This cluster insists on person-level interpretation and conceptual caution against reified variable-based accounts of personality.
theories.course_role: Acts as an internal critique of mainstream individual-difference psychology.

related_theories.rationale: Critical personalism argues that measurement cannot substitute for understanding the person.
Both oppose reductionism, though critical psychology is more sociohistorical.

representative_excerpts: Critical personalism (Forelæsning 15, 2026-03-23)
Critical personalism re-centers agency and context in personality theory.
Laux and colleagues discuss critical personalism as a framework for integrating person, action, and context.

theories.label: dynamic personality development
theories.summary: This cluster examines how personality patterns persist and shift through repeated interaction between persons, situations, and time.
theories.course_role: Keeps the differential block open to context, development, and process.
lecture_titles: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)

related_theories.rationale: Dynamic perspectives keep trait concepts but make them more process-sensitive.
Both stress context, though they frame it at different levels.

evidence_by_lecture.lecture_title: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)
representative_excerpts: Person-situation interplay - stability and change of individual differences (Forelæsning 4, 2026-02-10)
Li and Wilt analyze person-situation interplay in the expression and development of individual differences.

theories.label: personality functioning and pathology
theories.summary: This cluster frames pathology in terms of organisation, coherence, and impairment rather than only discrete categories.
theories.course_role: Bridges dimensional and clinical language before the psychoanalytic block.

related_theories.rationale: Questions of structure and organisation carry directly into the psychoanalytic sequence.

representative_excerpts: Sharp and Wall examine personality functioning and disturbance through dimensional, developmental lenses.
They discuss how impairments in self and interpersonal functioning relate to personality pathology.
Personality pathology is better treated dimensionally than categorically.
